assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with MIXED — both additive and reductive signals are present across the test file changes

assertion_results.text: The structural summary or reductive findings identify the removed test function test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers as a reductive signal

assertion_results.text: The structural summary or reductive findings note the assertion change in test_recommend_purls_basic — the PURL assertion changed from checking a fully qualified PURL with qualifiers to checking a versioned PURL without qualifiers, which the semantic assessment may identify as a relaxation contributing to the MIXED classification
assertion_results.evidence: Structural summary table lists '| test_recommend_purls_basic assertion | REDUCTIVE | Assertion relaxed from checking fully qualified PURL with qualifiers (pkg:maven/org.apache/commons-lang3@3.12?repository_url=https://repo1.maven.org&type=jar) to checking versioned PURL without qualifiers (pkg:maven/org.apache/commons-lang3@3.12). |' Reductive Findings section details: 'Relaxed assertion in test_recommend_purls_basic — The assertion assert_eq! was replaced... The old assertion verified the exact string including all qualifiers; the new assertion verifies only the type/namespace/name/version portion.' Semantic assessment explicitly identifies this as a relaxation contributing to MIXED.

Tests deduplication behavior after qualifier removal. 1 function added, 4 assertions. |' Combined signal tally: '1 function added in modified file (test_recommend_purls_dedup)'.

assertion_results.text: The test change classification verdict appears in the verification report summary table and is accompanied by a detailed analysis section explaining the structural and semantic assessment
assertion_results.evidence: Summary table row: '| Test Change Classification | MIXED | Both additive and reductive signals present...' Detailed analysis section titled 'Test Change Classification -- MIXED' follows with subsections: 'Structural Summary' (two file-level tables with per-function signal classification and combined signal tally), 'Semantic Assessment' (narrative explaining why MIXED is correct), and 'Reductive Findings' (two numbered items with specific details).

assertion_results.text: The test change classification analysis is based on comparing base-branch and PR-branch file content (function additions, removals, assertion changes) — the structural and semantic assessment references test file content, not acceptance criteria or task requirements (constraint 1.18)
assertion_results.evidence: Structural summary compares function-level changes: removed test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers, relaxed assertion text in test_recommend_purls_basic (quotes exact old and new assert_eq! values), added test_recommend_purls_dedup, lists unchanged functions (test_recommend_purls_unknown_returns_empty, test_recommend_purls_pagination). Semantic assessment discusses 'removed function verified a behavioral contract (qualifier preservation as distinct entries)', 'relaxed assertion weakens what was being verified', and 'new test_recommend_purls_dedup function tests entirely new deduplication behavior that had no equivalent on the base branch.' All references are to test file content (function names, assertion text, file paths), not to acceptance criteria or task requirements.
